evals.name: dual-dc-scenario-comparison
evals.prompt: We currently operate a single warehouse in Birmingham serving all of the UK and EU. We're considering adding a DC in Wroclaw, Poland. Current state: total logistics cost GBP 2.1M/yr, average transit 4.2 days, service level 91%. The Poland DC option is estimated at GBP 1.85M/yr logistics cost, 2.8 day avg transit, 96% service level, but requires GBP 800K capex. Our primary objective is service level, secondary is cost. Please compare these scenarios.
evals.expected_output: A network design analysis in the skill's defined format comparing the status quo against the Poland DC option, with scenario comparison table, payback calculation, trade-off analysis, sensitivity assessment, and specific next steps.
expectations: Produces the mandatory output header with TASK, CONFIGURATION, and DATA SOURCES fields — every network design output must begin with this header
Includes Scenario A (status quo baseline) alongside Scenario B (Poland DC) — the skill requires NEVER presenting a single scenario as a recommendation; always compare against the status quo
Shows all three dimensions (cost, service level, and carbon) in the scenario comparison — the skill requires NEVER optimising for cost alone without showing service and carbon implications
Calculates the payback period for the GBP 800K capex based on the GBP 250K/yr saving — the skill requires payback calculations with stated assumptions (discount rate, demand growth, cost escalation)
Includes a risks and sensitivities section addressing what assumptions could change the recommendation — network designs based on static assumptions fail when demand shifts or costs change
Provides specific next steps including site survey, regulatory checks, carrier RFQ, and approval requirements — the skill requires actionable implementation steps, not just analysis

evals.name: network-stress-test-disruption
evals.prompt: What happens if our sole supplier route through the Suez Canal is disrupted for 3 months? We currently ship 60% of our inbound materials via this route from our suppliers in South-East Asia. Our UK warehouse has about 6 weeks of safety stock for these materials. Model the contingency cost and tell me if our network has enough resilience.
evals.expected_output: A disruption scenario analysis modelling the contingency cost of Suez Canal closure, evaluating whether 6 weeks of safety stock covers the disruption period, identifying alternative routing options with cost and transit time impacts, and recommending resilience improvements.
expectations: Models the disruption as a specific scenario rather than giving generic advice — the skill supports conversational 'what if' scenarios and should quantify the impact
Evaluates the 6-week safety stock against the 3-month disruption period to identify the gap — 6 weeks covers less than half the disruption, so a material shortfall is expected
Identifies alternative supply routes (e.g., Cape of Good Hope) with estimated additional transit time and cost — contingency plans need quantified alternatives, not just 'consider alternatives'
Does NOT present only one scenario — the skill requires comparing the disruption scenario against the status quo baseline to quantify the cost of the disruption
Includes specific recommended resilience improvements with deadlines — observations without actions are not acceptable per the universal rules

evals.name: negative-carrier-performance-redirect
evals.prompt: Can you review our top 5 carriers' on-time delivery performance and create a quarterly scorecard? I want to see OTD rates, damage claims, cost per kg, and tracking reliability for each carrier.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this is a carrier performance review, not a network design question, and redirects to the logistics-brief skill.
expectations: Recognizes that carrier performance scorecards are logistics-brief skill territory — the skill description explicitly states 'NOT for: carrier performance (use logistics-brief)'

Redirects to the logistics-brief skill or /logistics-brief command — carrier scorecards use a different framework (OTD/damage/cost/tracking) than network design (facility placement and scenario modelling)
Does NOT attempt to produce a carrier performance scorecard — network design analyses supply chain topology, not individual carrier performance metrics

evals.name: negative-spend-analysis-redirect
evals.prompt: I want to analyze our total procurement spend across all categories and identify which vendors we should consolidate. Can you produce a spend-by-category breakdown with consolidation recommendations?
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this is a spend analysis request, not a network design question, and redirects to the spend-analysis skill.
expectations: Recognizes that spend-by-category analysis and vendor consolidation are spend-analysis skill territory — the skill description explicitly excludes spend category analysis
Redirects to the spend-analysis skill or /spend-analysis command — spend analysis has dedicated frameworks for category spend overview, vendor consolidation, and price benchmarking
Does NOT attempt to produce a spend analysis — network design handles facility placement and supply chain topology, not procurement spend aggregation
